channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: UN chief visits Haiti, where a new 'gang-suppression force' will be deployed
item.description: U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres's visit to Port-au-Prince comes as gang violence persists. According to U.N. data, 2,300 people have been killed in Haiti this year, with another 100 kidnapped.
item.content:encoded:
U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres's visit to Port-au-Prince comes as gang violence persists. According to U.N. data, 2,300 people have been killed in Haiti this year, with another 100 kidnapped.

(Image credit: Danica Coto)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. strike on an alleged drug boat kills 1, leaves 2 survivors
item.description: The U.S. military attacked a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Tuesday, killing one man and leaving two survivors. This brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes to at least 208.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military attacked a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Tuesday, killing one man and leaving two survivors. This brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes to at least 208.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rain along Gulf Coast could become the first named storm of Atlantic hurricane season
item.description: The National Hurricane Center in Miami says the system is expected to bring intense rain to southern states including Texas and Louisiana this week.
item.content:encoded:
The National Hurricane Center in Miami says the system is expected to bring intense rain to southern states including Texas and Louisiana this week.

(Image credit: NOAA via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Georgia results: Collins will face Sen. Ossoff; Trump's pick loses governor runoff
item.description: In an upset, Georgia Republican voters rejected President Trump's preferred nominee for the competitive open governor's race. They also picked Rep. Mike Collins to face Sen. Jon Ossoff.
item.content:encoded:
In an upset, Georgia Republican voters rejected President Trump's preferred nominee for the competitive open governor's race. They also picked Rep. Mike Collins to face Sen. Jon Ossoff.

(Image credit: Jason Allen)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Iraqi soccer fans celebrate end of 40-year World Cup drought
item.description: The Iraq national men's team hasn't played a World Cup in 40 years; a drought that ends Tuesday night, to the excitement of soccer fans in Dearborn, Michigan, home to a large Iraqi diaspora.
item.content:encoded:
The Iraq national men's team hasn't played a World Cup in 40 years; a drought that ends Tuesday night, to the excitement of soccer fans in Dearborn, Michigan, home to a large Iraqi diaspora.

(Image credit: Nick Hagen for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: In Albania, anger grows against the government for supporting a Kushner-linked luxury resort
item.description: Albania's government has given preliminary approval to plans for the luxury resort along a stretch of coastline, prompting daily protests and legal challenges by environmental groups.
item.content:encoded:
Albania's government has given preliminary approval to plans for the luxury resort along a stretch of coastline, prompting daily protests and legal challenges by environmental groups.

(Image credit: Vlasov Sulaj)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Trump further guts Education Dept. by shifting oversight of special ed, civil rights
item.description: The moves to the federal departments of Health and Human Services and Justice, respectively, would further dismantle an agency that President Donald Trump has vowed to close.
item.content:encoded:
The moves to the federal departments of Health and Human Services and Justice, respectively, would further dismantle an agency that President Donald Trump has vowed to close.

(Image credit: Al Drago)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: Live with a partner? You may be sharing more microbes than you think
item.description: A large study finds you may share about a quarter of your oral and gut microbes with the people you live with. Should you worry? We asked the experts.
item.content:encoded:
A large study finds you may share about a quarter of your oral and gut microbes with the people you live with. Should you worry? We asked the experts.

(Image credit: Benambot/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kim

item.title: The war with Iran is making oil changes pricier. And a deal won't solve it
item.description: The U.S. may be the world's biggest producer of crude oil, but that's not the case for motor oil. The cost of lubricants is soaring, and even a tentative deal to end the war won't solve the problem.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. may be the world's biggest producer of crude oil, but that's not the case for motor oil. The cost of lubricants is soaring, and even a tentative deal to end the war won't solve the problem.

(Image credit: Sergio Flores)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Pop albums are drowning in 'narrative.' What happens when we go in cold?
item.description: New albums by Lizzo and the rising artist Imani Imani are both "event" records — but one arrives with arguably too much backstory, the other with almost none.
item.content:encoded:
New albums by Lizzo and the rising artist Imani Imani are both "event" records — but one arrives with arguably too much backstory, the other with almost none.

(Image credit: Lizzo by Jason Renaud / Imani Imani courtesy of pgLang)
item.dc:creator: Sheldon Pearce
